_meta.description: Ubiquitous Language Glossary for SuperPersonal — the canonical authority for all identifiers used in production code.

terms.definition: A registered personal computer (the target PC) that the user controls remotely. Identified by a stable HardwareID generated on the client machine. The aggregate root for all commands issued to that machine.
not_to_be_confused_with: Client (the Python process running on the Device)
examples: Work Laptop that was last seen 5 minutes ago

terms.definition: A UUID generated once on first client startup and persisted locally on the target PC. Immutable after generation. Used to re-identify the same physical machine across restarts and IP changes.
not_to_be_confused_with: DeviceID (the server-side database primary key assigned at registration)

terms.definition: The server-assigned UUID primary key (pk_device_id) for a Device record in the database. Assigned at registration and never changes.
examples: UUID assigned by Django ORM on first POST /devices/register

terms.definition: A discrete instruction issued by the user via the web dashboard to be executed on a specific Device. Has a lifecycle: PENDING → SENT → QUEUED → RUNNING → SUCCESS | FAILED | CANCELLED. The aggregate root for the command execution lifecycle.
not_to_be_confused_with: CommandType (the classification of what a command does)
CommandExecutor (the client-side object that runs a command)
examples: A CMD_SET_VOLUME command with payload {level: 50} dispatched to the Gaming PC

terms.definition: An enumerated classification of a Command that determines which CommandExecutor handles it on the client. Values: CMD_PING, CMD_SHELL_EXEC, CMD_OPEN_BROWSER, CMD_SCREENSHOT, CMD_SYSTEM_INFO, CMD_LOCK_PC, CMD_SHUTDOWN, CMD_RESTART, CMD_SCHEDULED_SHUTDOWN, CMD_SET_VOLUME, CMD_OPEN_PRESET, CMD_MEDIA.

The current lifecycle state of a Command. Valid transitions: PENDING → SENT; SENT → QUEUED; QUEUED → RUNNING; RUNNING → SUCCESS | FAILED; any active state → CANCELLED. Invalid transitions must be rejected by the Command aggregate.

An immutable Value Object carrying the typed parameters for a Command. Fields are command-type-specific: url (CMD_OPEN_BROWSER), seconds (CMD_SCHEDULED_SHUTDOWN), level/mute (CMD_SET_VOLUME). Unknown fields are stored in extra{}.

An immutable Value Object produced by a CommandExecutor after execution. Contains the final CommandStatus, stdout output, and any error trace. Sent from Client to Server via POST /commands/{id}/result.
not_to_be_confused_with: CommandLog (the persisted record on the server)

terms.definition: The persistent server-side record of a Command's execution output. Created when the Client posts a result. Contains stdout output and error trace. One-to-one with Command.
not_to_be_confused_with: CommandResult (transient Value Object on the wire)

terms.definition: A client-side Domain Service (abstract base class) that encapsulates the OS-level logic for a specific CommandType. Each CommandType has exactly one registered CommandExecutor subclass. Stateless: no mutable instance fields after construction.
not_to_be_confused_with: ExecutorRegistry (the factory/registry that selects the right executor)

terms.definition: A Domain Service (registry/factory) on the client that maps CommandType to a concrete CommandExecutor. The sole point where CommandExecutor instances are selected. Uses the @register decorator pattern.
A user-saved shortcut pairing a name with a URL (e.g., a YouTube playlist). Issuing a CMD_OPEN_PRESET command opens the URL on the target Device. The aggregate root of the Preset Management context.
not_to_be_confused_with: Command (the act of opening a preset)

terms.definition: A domain concept representing a CMD_SCHEDULED_SHUTDOWN Command with a future execution time derived from a seconds-delay payload. The system must enforce that the delay is a positive integer.
examples: ScheduledShutdown with delay=3600 seconds triggers shutdown in 1 hour

terms.definition: A Value Object representing a point-in-time snapshot of a Device's hardware utilisation: CPU usage (%), RAM usage (%), and optionally disk/network. Produced by CMD_SYSTEM_INFO execution.
A derived Value Object representing whether a Device is currently reachable: ONLINE if last_seen within the heartbeat threshold (default 60 seconds), OFFLINE otherwise.
The ordered collection of PENDING Commands for a Device that has not yet acknowledged them. When a Device comes online, it drains its CommandQueue in FIFO order.
examples: CommandQueue for 'Gaming PC' with 3 queued commands waiting for the device to reconnect
